tape: Risk-off-but-bid into the close: S&P +0.4% / Nasdaq +0.9% on the coolest CPI in 6+ years (3.5% yoy, -0.4% m/m) and a Goldman blowout (+3%, EPS $20.98 vs $14.48), though JPM -2.5% and BAC -0.8% on their prints and IBM -25% on a dour preview cap the tech tape -- the live wildcard is the US Strait of Hormuz 20%-cargo-toll blockade that begins enforcing 4pm ET TODAY (UN/Rubio call it illegal 'piracy'), keeping research.json's risk_off call intact even with VIX ~17.2 and quant_regime reading calm_drift.

alerts.headline: NEW top-of-radar buy candidate (6.7) but AVOID: 12-for-1 reverse split effective TODAY (7/14) purely to hold Nasdaq $1 bid-price compliance (Rule 5550(a)(2)), on top of a pump into an unfunded 'AI + blockchain traceability + crypto/stablecoin payments' pivot -- shares whipsawed +130% (7/1), -39% (7/5), +27% AH. Reverse-split + crypto-pivot pump, not the clean insider fundamental the 6.7 score implies. NOTE: FinBERT's +0.919 on JEM is misattributed to a generic 'US Stocks Edge Higher / Delta' headline, not real JEM news -- disregard.

NEW top-of-radar buy candidate (6.6) but AVOID: textbook pump-and-dump signature -- HK event-management microcap ran from ~$0.23 (6/12) to >$10 intraday (6/16) and has since collapsed to the low-$4s (~$3.54). Extreme volatility, not a fundamental catalyst; the 6.6 is an insider-driven score on a name the bot's pump veto should reject.

NEW radar candidate (6.67): insider cluster is REAL and verifiable -- CEO Daniel de Boer bought 150,000 sh at $1.59 on 7/7 (open market) and non-exec director Bart Filius bought ~EUR231k, the biggest PRQR insider buy in a year. BUT overhang: an F-3 shelf plus a 7/6 resale prospectus for 3,030,303 sh sit against a $1.72 stock. Buys are ~1wk old and already the basis of the score -- no fresh catalyst, no boost; not a pump, so info not flag.

Held/top book gainer +7.2% (~$12.41); Q1 FY2027 rev $201.6M +28% yoy, Buy consensus, avg PT $16.00. Move is already in the tape, not a fresh un-priced catalyst -- and risk.json's post-lockup Lightspeed XII distribution (219,075 sh 7/9 after the 7/8 sale) remains a live overhang that Iconiq's $7.2M 7/8 buy only partly offsets.

Carried flag: still no confirmed active 424B5/ATM draw, but risk.json confirms a $200M universal S-3 baby-shelf IS on file -- standing dilution capacity on a sub-$100M post-+48%-pump name the bot's own critic vetoed 3x today. Stabilizing ~$2.12; keep on AVOID, do not add.

Held, no fresh news; CEO Bhappu $968k (7/7) + chairman Hansen (7/8) buys already known. Active $300M ATM (risk.json elevated) and Lorentzian k-NN -0.75 bearish still unaddressed by the insider-buy thesis; position soft.

Held, fundamentals clean (director $990k 7/13, Caris Detect launch, $100M buyback, no dilution). risk.json re-flags a persistent broker-vs-cache price drift (~3%, broker $17.50) -- P&L fine, stop math referencing the cache is off. Data issue, not fundamentals.

Watchlist: Equinor $1B+/7-rig-year backlog intact, but concur with risk.json -- the pending ~$5.8B all-stock Valaris acquisition (15.235 RIG/Valaris sh) diluting existing holders to ~53% of the combined co is a material binary-vote/dilution risk the 'clean backlog' framing understates. Not the clean trade research presents.
